free_text: Radiative proton capture into 14N
The targets were prepared by cracking 50%-55% 13C
enriched methyl iodide gas on heated metallic surfaces.
OG
The 13C targets were deposited on 500 mu-m Ta
foils. Thickness of target was 20 mu-g/cm2.
The Stanford 10 inch NaI assembly contained
an anti-coincidence shield for lineshape improvement
and the cosmic-ray rejection.
No information on uncertainties.
Fig.4 (p.468) in Nucl.Phys.A,175,462,1971 was
digitized by GSYS2.4.7.
(p,g0) and (p,g1) cross sections at
5-18 MeV
The 13C targets were deposited on 500 mu-m Ta
foils. Thickness of target was 20 mu-g/cm2.
The inelastic proton scattering to the first
three excited states in 13C was measured by
observation of the de-excitation gamma-rays in a 6
cm3 Ge(Li) detector.
No information on uncertainties.
Fig.4 (p.468) in Nucl.Phys.A,175,462,1971 was
digitized by GSYS2.4.7.
gamma yields at 6-17 MeV

The self-supporting targets, obtained by depositing
carbon onto polished Ni foils of 50 mu-m thickness,
were used. Thickness of targets were 150 mu-g/cm2.
The Stanford 10 inch NaI assembly contained
an anti-coincidence shield for lineshape improvement
and the cosmic-ray rejection.
No information on uncertainties.
